examples: For example, almost all Qatari women wear the black abaya (also donned in Saudi Arabia).
Some wearing traditional long black abaya, others clad in jeans and T-shirts, wait to see a female doctor.
Women in Saudi Arabia already have to wear a long black cloak, called an abaya, cover their hair and, in some regions, conceal their faces while in public.
Before the Gulf war, only religious Shia Muslim women wore the abaya, the traditional head-to-toe black cloak.
Right down to the security guard, they are all female and dressed in the traditional black abaya and sheila worn by most Gulf women.
